title: Presenter Change
description: Maps kernel or graph results into the matching `dto-*` response for **Presenter Change**. Presenters are pure formatting at the HTTP boundary so handlers do not embed lifecycle or validation logic.
context: Maps kernel or graph results into the matching `dto-*` response for **Presenter Change**. Presenters are pure formatting at the HTTP boundary so handlers do not embed lifecycle or validation logic.

rules.requirement: presenter maps entities to DTO fields deterministically
rules: Given the same kernel or graph result, the presenter MUST produce the same DTO JSON shape, omitting optional properties when values are absent.

rules.requirement: presenter does not encode business rules
rules: The presenter MUST NOT decide lifecycle transitions, validation outcomes, or approval state â€” those belong in `@specd/core` use cases.

`toChangeDetailDto` MUST map each kernel `ChangeEvent` to `ChangeHistoryEventDto` with ISO `at`, actor `by`, and all variant-specific properties needed by Studio (`ui:change-tab-events`). At minimum: `created` â†’ `specIds`, `schemaName`, `schemaVersion`; `transitioned` â†’ `from`, `to`; `invalidated` â†’ `cause`, `message`, `affectedArtifacts`. Other variants (`spec-approved`, `signed-off`, `artifacts-synced`, etc.) MUST forward their domain fields without dropping them to type-only stubs.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact â€” not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: presenter maps entities to DTO fields deterministically
scenarios.name: Presenter output matches paired DTO

scenarios.requirement: presenter maps entities to DTO fields deterministically
scenarios.name: Presenter leaves kernel blockers unchanged

scenarios.requirement: presenter maps entities to DTO fields deterministically
scenarios.name: Repeated mapping is deterministic
when: presenter called twice on same input
then: AND no time-dependent fields unless documented

scenarios.name: Invalidated event retains cause and affected artifacts
given: kernel history with invalidated and affectedArtifacts
then: JSON history entry includes cause, message, and affectedArtifacts
AND by uses name and email

scenarios.name: Spec-approved event retains hashes
then: history entry includes reason and artifactHashes
AND is not reduced to type/at/by only
